# Source: RDA -2010 for moderately active person The proportion of households with energy inadequacy was about 70%, while that with protein inadequacy was about 27%. Thus, in the cereal/millet-based Indian dietaries, the primary bottleneck is energy inadequacy and not the protein, as was earlier believed. This dietary energy gap can be easily reduced by the poor by increasing the quantities of habitually eaten foods.
